name: Qatar
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **QATAR FERTILIZER/UREA EXPORT NORMALIZATION EXPECTATION DRIVING CF -6.5% / NTR -4.4% TODAY (April 17 playbook rerun); MOU 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES' PROVISION INCLUDES IMPLICIT QATAR REOPENING.** Today's fertilizer rotation is the SECOND-cleanest peace-dividend trade after energy: forward-pricing of Qatar Ras Laffan / urea export normalization is repricing the scarcity premium. Urea generic at $577.50/T (May 5) drifting lower; Argus Q2 forecast $715 (Yuzhny FOB benchmark) has room to compress further. Egypt NCIC late-April spot $852/t represents the high-water mark. CF is now -10.5% on the month and tracking the symmetrical inverse of the Hormuz-closure scarcity rally that drove it +27% in 3 months. **Qatar's mediation role:** Doha continues supporting Pakistan as primary channel (Qatari FM al-Ansari: 'we don't need to expand the circle of negotiations'); Qatari PM/FM Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani held separate phone call with Iran's FM Araghchi on de-escalation. **Helium:** spot doubled since Feb 28 crisis start ($163.72/MC April per gasworld); Air Liquide reallocating supply away from ME-dependent regions. Qatar's structural relevance to the brief sits at the intersection of (a) Hormuz reopening would unlock LNG/fertilizer/helium supply, (b) Doha mediation supporting role, (c) Qatar's GCC alignment with Pakistan track.
